mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, triggering a positive equity risk premium and lifting S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.5, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and business sector demand expansion, which improves corporate revenue visibility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable inflation indicates a supply-driven productivity boom that expands profit margins without triggering monetary tightening, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% annually while CPIAUCSL remains flat, indicating that supply shocks are cost-push rather than productivity-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with upward earnings revisions for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 5-year window, or earnings revision momentum decouples from GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.25 percent triggers fears of premature monetary tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 60% of the time following CPI spikes above 0.25% MoM, or the negative return signal disappears after adjusting for FEDFUNDS rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and declining housing starts signals a structural shift toward services and away from capital-intensive construction, which historically precedes equity market corrections due to reduced investment flows.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while HOUST falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS and HOUST diverge as specified, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS and INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, surging retail sales amplify consumer spending momentum, which rapidly translates into higher corporate cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 returns within a single week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances during identified credit expansion periods, or RSAFS growth decouples from equity returns during liquidity crunches.
